name: Reynolds number — inertial / viscous similarity parameter
principles: dimensional analysis of the incompressible Navier-Stokes equations
dynamic similarity between geometrically similar flows
inertial momentum flux versus viscous momentum diffusion
conventions: rho is the fluid density (kg/m^3); U is a characteristic velocity (m/s)
L is a characteristic length (m) — pipe diameter, chord length, body size; the choice of L must be declared with the result
mu is the dynamic viscosity (Pa·s) and nu = mu / rho is the kinematic viscosity (m^2/s)
two flows with the same Re and the same boundary geometry are dynamically similar — non-dimensional velocity and pressure fields agree

Reynolds (1883) experimental boundary in straight smooth pipes: laminar Re < 2300; transitional 2300–4000; fully turbulent Re > 4000 (geometry-specific — flat plate, sphere, airfoil each have their own thresholds)
expectedLimits: Re -> 0 (Stokes / creeping flow): inertial term negligible, Navier-Stokes reduces to mu nabla^2 u = nabla p; drag on a sphere becomes F = 6 pi mu U R (Stokes drag)
Re -> infinity (inviscid limit): Navier-Stokes reduces to Euler equations away from boundary layers; viscous effects survive only in thin layers of thickness delta / L ~ Re^{-1/2}
pipe flow, Re < ~2300: laminar Hagen-Poiseuille velocity profile, parabolic, friction factor f = 64/Re
pipe flow, Re > ~4000: turbulent profile, Colebrook / Moody friction factor depends on relative roughness and Re
sphere drag, Re ~ 1: Stokes' formula valid; Re ~ 10^3-10^5: drag coefficient C_D ~ 0.4 nearly constant (drag crisis at Re ~ 3 x 10^5)
low-Re scaling for biological microswimmers (bacteria, sperm cells): Re ~ 10^-4-10^-2, fundamentally time-reversible kinematics (scallop theorem)
references: Reynolds (1883) — An Experimental Investigation of the Circumstances Which Determine Whether the Motion of Water Shall Be Direct or Sinuous, Phil. Trans. R. Soc.
Batchelor — An Introduction to Fluid Dynamics, ch.4
Purcell (1977) — Life at low Reynolds number, Am. J. Phys. 45 (3): 3-11
